caption: The video displays a static presentation slide for a course titled "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" specifically for "Tutorial – 1". The slide features a graph plotting yield $y$ against effectiveness $\varepsilon$, showing a positive upward curve. A red circle highlights a point on the x-axis. To the left of the graph, text lists the parameters: "Working Fluid : Air", "Pressure : 1 bar $\rightarrow$ 200 bar", "Temperature : 300 K", and "Linde – Hampson System". To the right, bullet points state, "Joining the points, we have the trend as shown in the figure.", "It is clear that as the effectiveness decreases, the yield $y$ decreases drastically.", and "Furthermore, the effectiveness should be more than 85% in order to have a liquid yield." The "NPTEL" logo is in the bottom left, and the bottom footer reads, "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay". The scene remains identical across all frames.
